human:
POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:136] > that part of the changeset isn't necessary to accomplish the stated goal of this issue.

It is unrelated.  Its a bug I noticed while working this issue.  The current code will not set an error return code for the process.
v7:
SUMMARY:
PR removes informational scan messages ("Scanning snapshot...", "No data found") and changes "Scan failed" error handling from `System.out.println` to `System.err.println` + `System.exit(-1)`, aligning with established conventions in the same file and across the codebase.

POINTS:
- [ScanUtil.java:141] Removing the "No data found" message may degrade user experience — when a scan returns zero results with the existing `System.out.checkError()` break condition, users will see no output at all and cannot distinguish between "no data" and "something went silently wrong." Consider keeping this message or returning a distinct exit code.
- [ScanUtil.java:90-93] The informational messages ("Scanning snapshot of data..." / "Scanning data in Accumulo directly...") being removed are useful user feedback, especially since scan operations can take noticeable time. Without them, users see no indication the command started. This may be intentional (cleaner output), but consider documenting in the PR description why these were removed.
- [ScanUtil.java:141,171] Changing `System.out.println("Scan failed - ...")` to `System.err.println(...)` + `System.exit(-1)` is a **positive change** that aligns with the pattern already used in the same file for `IllegalArgumentException` (lines 105-107, 158-160 in base) and consistently across CLI commands (`FluoExec`, `FluoInit`, `BaseOpts`). Verified both callers (`FluoScan.main()` and deprecated `AppRunner.scan()`) are appropriate contexts for `System.exit()`.
- [ScanUtil.java:141] The exit code is `-1` which on most Unix systems becomes `255` (since exit codes are 8-bit unsigned). This is acceptable but note it doesn't match `System.exit(0)` used for success in `AppRunner.scan()` for help display. Minor inconsistency with `FluoWait.java` which uses `System.exit(-1)` without documentation of the intended convention.
- [ScanUtil.java:90] The whitespace-only changes in the Apache license header (trailing whitespace removal on lines 7,10) are fine but could be a separate cleanup commit to keep the PR focused on the functional changes.
v8:
SUMMARY:
This PR removes informational print statements from `scanFluo` and `scanAccumulo`, and improves error handling by routing errors to `System.err` with a non-zero exit code. The PR title is contradictory to the actual changes — it claims to fix meta info that "stopped printing" but the diff removes meta info rather than restoring it.

POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java] The PR title "fixes #981 stopped printing meta info in scan" contradicts the diff. The changes REMOVE the "Scanning snapshot of data..." banner and "No data found" message. Either the title should be rewritten to reflect that meta info is being intentionally removed, or the changes should be reconsidered if #981 was about restoring lost output.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:130] (removed lines 130-131 in base) Removing the `entriesFound` counter and the "No data found" message is a user experience regression. Previously, users received clear feedback when a scan returned zero results. Without this, empty output is ambiguous — users cannot distinguish between "no matching data" and "something went wrong silently." Consider retaining the "No data found" message even if the counter is removed.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:135-136] Changing `System.out.println("Scan failed ...")` to `System.err.println(...)` with `System.exit(-1)` is an improvement. The old behavior printed errors to stdout and returned with exit code 0, which incorrectly signaled success to the shell. The new behavior is consistent with `AppRunner.scan()` which already uses `System.exit(-1)` for error conditions (e.g., lines 67, 72).

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:170-171] Same error handling improvement applies to `scanAccumulo` — good change.

- Both callers (`FluoScan.main()` and `AppRunner.scan()`) are CLI entry points that do nothing after calling the scan methods, so `System.exit(-1)` is safe and appropriate here.

- No existing tests cover `scanFluo` or `scanAccumulo` (`ScanTest.java` only tests `getSpan` and `getColumns`). The behavioral changes in this PR are untested, though this is a pre-existing gap rather than something introduced by this PR.
